item.title: A guide to Hong Kong’s best under-the-radar art spaces
item.description: From pop-ups and residencies to cross-gallery ‘knots’, an alternative scene thrives alongside the city’s blue-chip galleries

item.title: Recession by Tyler Goodspeed — a myth-busting guide to economic contractions
item.description: A former head of the Council of Economic Advisers explores how and why downturns happen

item.title: Can AI help me find a home?
item.description: New technology is transforming house-hunting — but will Machine vision and Gaussian splats replace portals and estate agents?

item.title: Trump’s war will boost the clean energy sector he detests
item.description: Turmoil in oil and gas markets is pushing governments to seek alternatives

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Escalates Attacks in Lebanon as Iran Strikes Near Tel Aviv
item.description: Israel carried out deadly strikes in Beirut and other Lebanese cities, intensifying its assault against Iran-backed Hezbollah militants. Tehran retaliated for the killing of its de facto leader with missile strikes that left two dead in Israel.

item.title: Middle East Dispatch newsletter: Iran’s mood shifts
item.description: The immediate imperative is no longer political change but personal safety, writes Nick Pelham, our Middle East correspondent

item.title: Nigeria president begins first UK state visit in 37 years
item.description: King Charles and senior royals will provide a ceremonial welcome for Nigeria's President Tinubu on his state visit.

item.title: Over 200 Ukrainian Experts in Middle East to Help Fight Drones, Zelensky Says
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky said that 11 countries in the region have asked for Kyiv’s assistance. Russia has launched such drones against Ukraine for years.

item.title: Hospital attack in heart of Kabul shatters hopes of peace
item.description: Many fear escalation as China-brokered shuttle diplomacy fails to ease tensions

item.title: Cyborg cockroaches are coming to a pipeline near you
item.description: The remote-controlled insects are equipped with cameras to search disaster zones and check for infrastructure damage

item.title: Here’s the latest.
Four challenges for BHP’s incoming chief
item.description: World’s biggest miner taps ‘safe pair of hands’ to navigate copper shift, M&A speculation and tough China environment

item.title: A fashion getaway in Mexico City
item.description: Raise the temperature in strong stripes, bright colours and ruffled textures

item.title: Israel Intensifies Strikes on Beirut, Escalating Its Campaign Against Hezbollah
item.description: The Israeli military widened its attacks to the districts in the center of the Lebanese capital, destroying buildings, forcing residents to flee and killing at least 10.

item.title: FirstFT: Joe Kent resignation highlights Maga divisions over Iran war
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Microsoft considers legal action against OpenAI and Amazon

item.title: ‘Steroid Olympics’ company to start selling peptides after Kennedy signals deregulation
item.description: Enhanced Games, backed by Donald Trump Jr and Peter Thiel, is moving into consumer products

item.title: Rayner warns immigration reforms risk being 'un-British'
item.description: Most migrant workers will have to wait longer to qualify for permanent residence under the government's proposals.

item.title: What Reeves misses in pursuit of ‘securonomics’
item.description: UK is too slow to boost defence spending while underfunding areas of comparative advantage

item.title: The Faroe Islands Are Changing Some of Europe’s Strictest Abortion Rules
item.description: A new law allowing abortion up to 12 weeks will be a major shift in an archipelago of 55,000 people, and there are strong feelings on both sides.

item.title: He Lost a Leg for Russia. Then, He Says, His Country Betrayed Him.
item.description: Imprisoned for murder, Aleksandr Abbasov-Derskhan sought a new start in life and freedom by signing up to fight in Ukraine. But he says promised benefits proved illusory.

item.title: Analysis: Rayner's explosive speech reignites leadership speculation
item.description: The ex-deputy PM did not name Keir Starmer in her attack on Labour's direction and policies - but she did not have to.

item.title: Betting on Ayatollah’s Ouster Ignites Ire Over Prediction Markets
item.description: Bettors are angry that Kalshi, citing a policy against death bets, did not pay on wagers. And with signs of trading on government secrets, some lawmakers want to restrict prediction markets.

item.title: As Iran War Drags On, Europe Wants to Avoid a New Migration Crisis
item.description: A decade ago, a surge in migration to Europe spurred the far-right’s rise. European leaders now fear the Iran war could set off another crisis, and they have taken tentative steps to prepare.

item.title: Netanyahu Hopes Strikes on Iran Will Lead to Uprising and Regime Change
item.description: Israeli attacks have targeted the command centers of Iran’s repressive, internal security forces in hopes that Iranians will overthrow their rulers. Some see that as wishful thinking.

item.title: Ukraine faces missile shortage due to Middle East war, says Zelensky
item.description: In an exclusive interview with the BBC, the Ukrainian president says Putin wants a "long war" between the US and Iran.

item.title: Rayner warns Starmer his government is ‘running out of time’
item.description: Former deputy prime minister’s intervention comes ahead of local election in May

item.title: Dozens Dead in Pakistani Airstrike on Kabul
item.description: The attack hit a drug rehabilitation facility, and Afghanistan vowed to retaliate, risking an escalation of the conflict between the two countries.

item.title: K-shaped consumer credit
item.description: Plus, investing in the Ukraine build-out

item.title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
item.description: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.

item.title: EU countries see echoes of Syrian migration crisis in Iran war fears
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Europe’s Greens make a pitch to join the centrist coalition

item.title: Chinese national charged for trying to smuggle 2,000 ants from Kenya
item.description: Zhang Kequn was found with the large consignment of ants in his luggage at a Nairobi airport.

item.title: Venezuela beats Team USA to claim World Baseball Classic
item.description: The Venezuelans defeated Team USA 3-2 in Miami on Tuesday, in a thrilling final.

item.title: Will the Iran war squeeze India's piped gas next?
item.description: The Iran war has already rattled LPG users. Will India’s piped gas network face the next squeeze?

item.title: David Zaslav, once upon a time in Hollywood
item.description: The Warner Bros boss could get a $700mn payday from the sale of his studio to Paramount

item.title: Trump has broken it. Now he owns it
item.description: Continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens the movement of gas and fertilisers as well as oil

item.title: Iran still seen capable of escalating attacks on Gulf’s energy
item.description: Analysts warn Tehran’s ability to prolong disruption to oil and gas flows ‘has a way to go’

item.title: Iran conflict turns shipping market into ‘wild west’
item.description: Containers are being dropped off at far-flung ports while freight rates have soared

item.title: Banks prepare to offload $18bn in debt tied to EA take-private deal
item.description: Blockbuster junk bond and loan offering will test investor appetite at a time of heightened nerves over AI disruptions

item.title: Now it’s Paramount that faces one battle after another
item.description: Media mogul David Ellison has bagged the prize — but the deal needs to work for shareholders

item.title: How TrumpRx Drug Prices Compare With Those in Other Countries
item.description: The TrumpRx website claims to offer the best prices for medications. Here’s where Americans still pay more — and much more.

item.title: Trump’s Friendship With Japan’s Leader Takaichi Faces Test Over Iran
item.description: In Washington on Thursday, President Trump is expected to press Sanae Takaichi for military help in the Strait of Hormuz. But she faces constraints on what she can offer.

item.title: Morocco awarded Afcon title after Caf overturns result
item.description: Morocco declared the winners of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations after the Confederation of African Football overturns the result of their final defeat by Senegal.

item.title: Suspected stray bullet from military drill hits girl at playground
item.description: South Korea's military has since halted all shooting drills involving small firearms.

item.title: As American Bombers Take Off From R.A.F. Fairford in UK, Iraq War Looms Large
item.description: R.A.F. Fairford was the site of repeated antiwar protests during the Iraq war in 2003. Now it is being used again as a base for U.S. bombing missions in the Middle East.

item.title: India's cheap weight-loss drugs could reshape global obesity fight
item.description: With India’s semaglutide patent expiring on 20 March, about 50 branded generics are expected to enter the market.

item.title: Scotland's assisted dying bill rejected after emotional debate
item.description: Scotland would have become the first part of the UK to legalise the process had MSPs backed the proposals.

item.title: Australia shock jock Kyle Sandilands sacked and top-rating show cancelled
item.description: It comes after an on-air spat between Sandilands and his co-host Jackie 'O' Henderson a month ago.

item.title: Bodo/Glimt's fairytale run comes to crashing halt
item.description: Bodo/Glimt's dream run in this season's Champions League may be over, but they bow out with their heads held high after making history.

item.title: What to Know About Ali Larijani, Iran’s Top Security Official
item.description: One of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s closest confidants, Mr. Larijani emerged as the face of the government after Mr. Khamenei was killed.

item.title: Watch: Sean Penn receives 'Oscar' in Ukraine after skipping US ceremony
item.description: The Academy Award winning US actor won his third Oscar on Sunday, but skipped the ceremony to visit Ukraine.

item.title: Italy warns Russian tanker could explode in Mediterranean at any time
item.description: Italy is one of nine EU countries urging the European Commission to act over the Arctic Metagaz, drifting out of control in the Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Inside the Supply Line Delivering American Guns to Mexican Cartels
item.description: A surge of weapons is flowing from the U.S. to Mexico. These firearms — sourced from gun shops, shows, websites and apps — are funneled across the border to fuel the country’s most violent crimes.

item.title: Spanish king reopens debate on conquest of Mexico by acknowledging 'abuse'
item.description: King Felipe appears to have helped thaw frosty relations with Mexico while reviving a fierce debate over events 500 years ago.

item.title: Air strike hit Kabul rehab centre as patients ate dinner, survivor tells BBC
item.description: The exact number killed in Monday night's strike by Pakistan is still not known, but is feared to be in the hundreds.

item.title: Mayors to gain more spending power under Reeves tax plans
item.description: The chancellor sets out the government's plan for economic growth, which also includes closer ties to the EU.

item.title: Iran and Russia are 'brothers in hatred', Zelensky tells MPs
item.description: The president highlights how Tehran is selling drones to Moscow that have been used against Ukraine.

item.title: Africa’s richest man has ambitious plans for the continent
item.description: But the tycoon also has his critics

item.title: What have US allies and China said about sending ships to the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: US allies have either pushed back or remained cautious on Trump's initial request to send ships to the crucial oil route.

item.title: Are there enough missile interceptors?
item.description: Shortages in Israel and the Gulf could determine the war’s course

item.title: Water supply cut in Moldova after oil spill blamed on Russian strike in Ukraine
item.description: Moldova has imposed a 15-day state of alert in the Dniester river basin as a precaution.

item.title: Ministers still wrangling over delayed defence investment plan
item.description: MPs ask defence bosses why a vital investment plan promised before Christmas is still on hold.

item.title: Chile's president begins building border barrier less than week into term
item.description: José Antonio Kast visited the ditch - a few feet wide and deep - on Monday, calling it a "milestone".

item.title: Belgian court clears way for trial over 1961 killing of Congo PM Lumumba
item.description: Etienne Davignon, a 93-year-old ex-diplomat, is accused of involvement in the assassination of the independence hero.

item.title: As Carney Seeks Canadian Alliances with China, India and Qatar, He Looks Away on Human Rights
item.description: Canada’s prime minister chooses pragmatism in a turbulent world, which means doing business with countries that do not share Canada’s democratic values. Some critics see this as weakness.

item.title: Reform pledges to scrap VAT and green levies on energy bills
item.description: There are fears the war in Iran could lead to a surge in household energy bills.

item.title: Trump Signals He May Intervene in Cuba Following Iran and Venezuela
item.description: Still at war with Iran and in control in Venezuela, President Trump is signaling that he is about to intervene in another country.

item.title: Unite fined £265k for 'slow walk' bin strike protests
item.description: A judge rules the union repeatedly breached an injunction preventing the blocking of waste vehicles.

item.title: Reform council leader narrowly survives no confidence vote
item.description: Warwickshire County Council leader George Finch survived by one vote.

item.title: Kerr winner sends Australia into Asian Cup final
item.description: Captain Sam Kerr scores the winner as Australia beat China 2-1 to reach the final of the Asian Cup.

item.title: Easter holidaymakers switching from Dubai to Spain as flights fill up
item.description: It comes after the war in Iran caused mass disruption to flights across the Middle East and UAE.

item.title: Aspiring female Zambian politicians were asked for sexual favours, official says
item.description: These reports harm attempts to close the wide gender representation gap in Zambia, activists say.

item.title: Sucked Into War, Gulf Countries Face the Limits of U.S. Security Guarantees
item.description: Barraged by Iranian attacks and questioning the value of security ties with the United States, nations in the Gulf have turned to Ukraine, Australia and Italy for help.

item.title: Suspected suicide attacks kill at least 23 in north-east Nigeria
item.description: Multiple blasts struck some of the Maiduguri's busiest locations on Monday evening.

item.title: At Least 23 Killed in Nigeria as Insurgent Attacks Go On Despite U.S. Help
item.description: A teaching hospital and two markets in the city of Maiduguri were hit in what a military spokesman said were suicide bombings by Boko Haram.

item.title: Trump seeks to delay meeting with Xi in China
item.description: The escalating war in Iran has overshadowed many of the US' foreign policy objectives

item.title: Millions without electricity as Cuba's power grid collapses
item.description: Cuba's chronic fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US blockade on oil shipments to the island.

item.title: UN receives reports alleging torture of detainees in Venezuela continues
item.description: The UN is deeply concerned by allegations there have been incidents of torture after Nicolás Maduro's ouster.

item.title: Trump 'not happy' with UK response to Iran conflict
item.description: It comes after Sir Keir Starmer said the UK was working with allies on a plan to protect the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Arsenal trial and self-doubt: Valverde's journey to Real legend
item.description: Rejected by Arsenal as a teenager, Federico Valverde has transformed himself into a Real Madrid legend - and was the main man in their Champions League win over Manchester City last week.

item.title: Family of alleged Bondi gunmen have received death threats, court hears
item.description: Naveed Akram is seeking a court order to suppress his family's identity over fears for their safety.

item.title: Young Venezuelans voice hope and frustration as post-Maduro future unfolds
item.description: Young people who have only known one political system are adjusting to change after US forces seized Maduro.

item.title: Why did only two Iranian football players stay in Australia?
item.description: After Australia gave humanitarian visas to seven team members, only two decided to stay - the BBC's Katy Watson explains what happened.

item.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
item.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

item.title: Sri Lanka declares Wednesdays off as Asian countries try to conserve fuel
item.description: This is the latest in a series of measures undertaken by Asian countries which rely on oil from the Gulf.

item.title: India's outsourcing industry is worth $300bn. Can it survive AI?
item.description: Indian IT stocks have plunged as fears grow of AI disrupting back offices. But some say these are overblown.

item.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
item.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

item.title: 'Fire came from the sky and burned them' - life on the brink of civil war in South Sudan
item.description: Some 50,000 people are told to flee their homes as the army warns of a huge military offensive in Jonglei state.

item.title: Starmer announces £53m support to help with heating oil costs
item.description: The money will be for "vulnerable" households who have faced a sharp rise in energy bills since the outbreak of the US-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: The quiet recovery of Ireland’s ancient tongue
item.description: In the land of St Patrick, Irish is making a steady comeback

item.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Two charged in death of anti-Iranian regime activist in Canada
item.description: Police say the motive remains under investigation but noted the three had disputes on social media.

item.title: Fifa sanctions Nigeria and DR Congo over World Cup play-off
item.description: Fifa fines the Nigeria Football Federation and DR Congo Football Association for separate disciplinary breaches by supporters.

item.title: Starmer warned over assisted dying bill by more than 100 Labour MPs
item.description: More than 100 Labour MPs warn that failing to pass the legislation would undermine trust in politics.

item.title: 'My mother cried out one last time': Palestinian boy, 12, describes how Israeli forces killed his family in car
item.description: Khaled Bani Odeh's parents and two brothers were shot dead as they drove home from a shopping trip in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
item.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

item.title: Will America’s Asian allies get dragged into the Iran war?
item.description: They are worried Uncle Sam will abandon them if they don’t fight

item.title: Mexico City breaks Guinness Record with giant football training session
item.description: Mexico City set a Guinness record for the largest football training session as 9,500 participants filled the capital’s Zocalo square ahead of World Cup.

item.title: The War Room newsletter: A conflict Trump was ill-prepared for
item.description: There is no simple way to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, writes Shashank Joshi, our defence editor

item.title: A Maoist survival guide to the Iranian energy crisis
item.description: For a self-reliant power like China, no supplier is ever irreplaceable

item.title: Labour calls for tax investigation into Reform's Tice
item.description: It follows a newspaper report that Tice had avoided corporation tax worth hundreds of thousands of pounds.

item.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
item.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

item.title: Companies offered £3,000 to hire jobless under-24s
item.description: It comes as ministers grapple with spiralling unemployment rates among young people.

item.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
item.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

item.title: Ecuador deploys 75,000 soldiers and police to combat drug gangs
item.description: Citizens of the most violent-wracked provinces have been warned the government is "at war" with the gangs.

item.title: French national jailed for 10 years in Azerbaijan for spying
item.description: Martin Ryan was accused of collecting secret information about Baku's military co-operation with Turkey and Pakistan.

item.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
item.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

item.title: Far-left and far-right gains throw French mainstream parties into a quandary
item.description: Awkward choices emerge for mainstream parties after the first round of mayoral elections.

item.title: India hopeful Iran talks will ease Hormuz route for its ships
item.description: Foreign Minister S Jaishankar told FT there was no "blanket arrangement" for Indian ships to pass through the strait.

item.title: Meta and TikTok let harmful content rise after evidence outrage drove engagement, say whistleblowers
item.description: Companies allowed more harmful content on user’s feeds, knowing their algorithms ran on outrage, BBC hears.

item.title: Ten killed in fire at India hospital intensive care unit
item.description: All the victims of the fire in Odisha state are patients, and 11 hospital staff are being treated for burns.

item.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
item.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

item.title: How did Oscar winner Kpop Demon Hunters win hearts the world over?
item.description: We find out how the award-winning, demon-busting crooners have cast a spell world over.

item.title: One-off £2,000 grant gives care leavers head start, study finds
item.description: Participants were less likely to become homeless or spend time in hospital or prison, researchers say.

item.title: Fears of two-tier health system as more turn to private care, says watchdog
item.description: The patient watchdog warns of two-tier service as polling shows numbers paying for care is on the rise.

item.title: Is this product 'human-made'? The race to establish an AI-free logo
item.description: The backlash to the growing use of the tech has led to an explosion in attempts to come up with 'AI-Free' logo that could be used globally.

item.title: Ukraine's urgent fight on the financial frontline
item.description: The war-torn country is battling to secure crucial funding from the IMF and EU, as well as putting up taxes.

item.title: 'We will go wherever they hide': Rooting out IS in Somalia
item.description: Somalia became a key part of IS's global network after fighters were driven out of strongholds in Syria and Iraq.

item.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
item.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

item.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
item.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

item.title: Why the Iran crisis caught Europe flat-footed
item.description: The EU suffers the war’s fallout, but can do little to cut it short

item.title: The Iran war may be about to escalate
item.description: Gulf states could join the conflict

item.title: Open-source intelligence shuts down
item.description: Satellite images of the Middle East are suddenly disappearing

item.title: What Nitish Kumar did for Bihar, India’s poorest state
item.description: The state’s longest-serving chief minister raised voters’ expectations of their politicians

item.title: UK must build own nuclear missiles to end reliance on US, say Lib Dems
item.description: Sir Ed Davey is calling on the government to start work on a "fully independent British nuclear deterrent".
